text_devanagari:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
text_iast:
karmaṇy-evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana |
mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi ||
translation_en: You have the right to action alone, never to its fruits. Let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor let attachment bind you to inaction.

text_devanagari:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि-
न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥
text_iast:
na jāyate mriyate vā kadācin
nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ |
ajo nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṁ purāṇo
na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre ||
translation_en: The Self is never born, nor does it die. It has not come into being, nor will it cease to be. Unborn, eternal, ever-existing, primeval — it is not slain when the body is slain.

text_devanagari:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
text_iast:
yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata |
abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṁ sṛjāmy aham ||
translation_en: Whenever dharma declines and adharma rises, O Bharata, I manifest myself.

text_devanagari:
उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्।
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः॥
text_iast:
uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet |
ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ ||
translation_en: Lift yourself by your own self; do not let yourself sink. The self alone is the friend of the self, and the self alone is its enemy.

text_devanagari:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्॥
text_iast:
ananyāś cintayanto māṁ ye janāḥ paryupāsate |
teṣāṁ nityābhiyuktānāṁ yoga-kṣemaṁ vahāmy aham ||
translation_en: To those who think of me with unwavering devotion and constantly worship me — to these ever-united souls, I personally bring what they lack and protect what they have.

text_devanagari:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
text_iast:
sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja |
ahaṁ tvā sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ ||
translation_en: Abandon all duties and take refuge in me alone. I shall liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.

text_devanagari:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते।
सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते॥
text_iast:
dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate |
saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho 'bhijāyate ||
translation_en: Brooding on objects of the senses gives rise to attachment; from attachment springs desire; from thwarted desire, anger is born.

text_devanagari:
ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।
मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति॥
text_iast:
mamaivāṁśo jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ |
manaḥ-ṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛti-sthāni karṣati ||
translation_en: An eternal fragment of my own being becomes the living soul in this world, drawing to itself the six senses — including the mind — that abide in nature.

text_devanagari:
यदि देहं पृथक् कृत्य चिति विश्राम्य तिष्ठसि।
अधुनैव सुखी शान्तो बन्धमुक्तो भविष्यसि॥
text_iast:
yadi dehaṁ pṛthak kṛtya citi viśrāmya tiṣṭhasi |
adhunaiva sukhī śānto bandha-mukto bhaviṣyasi ||
translation_en: If you separate yourself from the body and rest in pure consciousness, even now you will become happy, peaceful, and free from bondage.

text_devanagari:
मुक्ताभिमानी मुक्तो हि बद्धो बद्धाभिमान्यपि।
किंवदन्तीह सत्येयं या मतिः सा गतिर्भवेत्॥
text_iast:
muktābhimānī mukto hi baddho baddhābhimāny api |
kiṁvadantīha satyeyaṁ yā matiḥ sā gatir bhavet ||
translation_en: He who thinks himself free is free; he who thinks himself bound remains bound. Here the saying is proved true: as one thinks, so one becomes.

text_devanagari:
आत्मा साक्षी विभुः पूर्ण एको मुक्तश्चिदक्रियः।
असङ्गो निःस्पृहः शान्तो भ्रमात्संसारवानिव॥
text_iast:
ātmā sākṣī vibhuḥ pūrṇa eko muktaś cid akriyaḥ |
asaṅgo niḥspṛhaḥ śānto bhramāt saṁsāravān iva ||
translation_en: The Self is witness, all-pervading, perfect, one, free, pure consciousness, actionless, unattached, desireless, at peace — and only seems caught in the world through delusion.

text_devanagari:
स वै पुंसां परो धर्मो यतो भक्तिरधोक्षजे।
अहैतुक्यप्रतिहता ययात्मा सुप्रसीदति॥
text_iast:
sa vai puṁsāṁ paro dharmo yato bhaktir adhokṣaje |
ahaituky apratihatā yayātmā suprasīdati ||
translation_en: That alone is the supreme dharma for all beings, by which arises devotion to the Lord beyond the senses — devotion that is causeless and unbroken, in which the self finds full peace.

text_devanagari:
न साधयति मां योगो न साङ्ख्यं धर्म उद्धव।
न स्वाध्यायस्तपस्त्यागो यथा भक्तिर्ममोर्जिता॥
text_iast:
na sādhayati māṁ yogo na sāṅkhyaṁ dharma uddhava |
na svādhyāyas tapas tyāgo yathā bhaktir mamorjitā ||
translation_en: Neither yoga, nor analysis, nor duty, O Uddhava, nor scripture-study, austerity, or renunciation reaches me as surely as fervent devotion does.

text_devanagari:
नमामीशमीशान निर्वाणरूपं
विभुं व्यापकं ब्रह्मवेदस्वरूपम्।
निजं निर्गुणं निर्विकल्पं निरीहं
चिदाकाशमाकाशवासं भजेऽहम्॥
text_iast:
namāmīśam īśāna nirvāṇa-rūpaṁ
vibhuṁ vyāpakaṁ brahma-veda-svarūpam |
nijaṁ nirguṇaṁ nirvikalpaṁ nirīhaṁ
cid-ākāśam ākāśa-vāsaṁ bhaje 'ham ||
translation_en: I bow to Lord Ishana, the very form of liberation — all-pervading, the essence of Veda and Brahman; the inherent, attributeless, undivided, motionless one — the sky of consciousness who dwells in space — him I worship.

text_devanagari:
कर्पूरगौरं करुणावतारं
संसारसारं भुजगेन्द्रहारम्।
सदा वसन्तं हृदयारविन्दे
भवं भवानीसहितं नमामि॥
text_iast:
karpūra-gauraṁ karuṇāvatāraṁ
saṁsāra-sāraṁ bhujagendra-hāram |
sadā vasantaṁ hṛdayāravinde
bhavaṁ bhavānī-sahitaṁ namāmi ||
translation_en: I bow to Bhava — white as camphor, the very embodiment of compassion, the essence of existence, garlanded by the king of serpents — ever dwelling in the lotus of the heart, together with Bhavani.

text_devanagari:
इन्द्रं मित्रं वरुणमग्निमाहु-
रथो दिव्यः स सुपर्णो गरुत्मान्।
एकं सद्विप्रा बहुधा वदन्त्य्
अग्निं यमं मातरिश्वानमाहुः॥
text_iast:
indraṁ mitraṁ varuṇam agnim āhur
atho divyaḥ sa suparṇo garutmān |
ekaṁ sad viprā bahudhā vadanty
agniṁ yamaṁ mātariśvānam āhuḥ ||
translation_en: They call it Indra, Mitra, Varuna, Agni, and the celestial bird Garutman. The truth is one; the wise call it by many names — Agni, Yama, Matarishvan.

text_devanagari:
असतो मा सद्गमय।
तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय।
मृत्योर्मामृतं गमय॥
text_iast:
asato mā sad gamaya |
tamaso mā jyotir gamaya |
mṛtyor māmṛtaṁ gamaya ||
translation_en: From the unreal, lead me to the real; from darkness, lead me to light; from death, lead me to immortality.

text_devanagari:
ईशावास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्।
तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम्॥
text_iast:
īśāvāsyam idaṁ sarvaṁ yat kiñca jagatyāṁ jagat |
tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā gṛdhaḥ kasya svid dhanam ||
translation_en: All this — whatever moves in this moving world — is pervaded by the Lord. Enjoy through renunciation; covet no one's wealth.

text_devanagari:
सत्यमेव जयते नानृतं
सत्येन पन्था विततो देवयानः।
text_iast:
satyam eva jayate nānṛtaṁ
satyena panthā vitato deva-yānaḥ |
translation_en: Truth alone triumphs, never falsehood; by truth is laid out the divine path.

text_devanagari:
ॐ सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै।
तेजस्विनावधीतमस्तु मा विद्विषावहै॥
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥
text_iast:
oṁ saha nāv avatu | saha nau bhunaktu |
saha vīryaṁ karavāvahai |
tejasvināv adhītam astu mā vidviṣāvahai ||
oṁ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ ||
translation_en: May He protect us both. May He nourish us both. May we work together with great vigor. May our study be illumined; may we never harbor ill will. Om peace, peace, peace.

text_devanagari:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः।
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत॥
text_iast:
mātrā-sparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkha-dāḥ |
āgamāpāyino 'nityās tāṁs titikṣasva bhārata ||
translation_en: The contacts of the senses with their objects give rise to cold and heat, pleasure and pain. They come and go — impermanent. Endure them, O Bharata.

text_devanagari:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः॥
text_iast:
nainaṁ chindanti śastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ |
na cainaṁ kledayanty āpo na śoṣayati mārutaḥ ||
translation_en: Weapons cannot cut it, fire cannot burn it, water cannot wet it, and wind cannot dry it.

text_devanagari:
प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः।
अहंकारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते॥
text_iast:
prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ |
ahaṅkāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate ||
translation_en: All actions are performed by the gunas of nature alone. One whose mind is deluded by ego thinks: 'I am the doer.'

text_devanagari:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे॥
text_iast:
paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṁ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām |
dharma-saṁsthāpanārthāya sambhavāmi yuge yuge ||
translation_en: For the protection of the righteous, for the destruction of the wicked, and to firmly establish dharma — I am born in age after age.

text_devanagari:
विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि।
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः॥
text_iast:
vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini |
śuni caiva śva-pāke ca paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ ||
translation_en: The wise see with equal vision a learned brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste.

text_devanagari:
चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद् दृढम्।
तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम्॥
text_iast:
cañcalaṁ hi manaḥ kṛṣṇa pramāthi balavad dṛḍham |
tasyāhaṁ nigrahaṁ manye vāyor iva suduṣkaram ||
translation_en: The mind is restless, turbulent, powerful, and obstinate, O Krishna. To restrain it seems to me as difficult as restraining the wind.

text_devanagari:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान् मां प्रपद्यते।
वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः॥
text_iast:
bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyate |
vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ ||
translation_en: At the end of many births, the man of wisdom surrenders to me, knowing Vasudeva is all. Such a great soul is very rare.

text_devanagari:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति।
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः॥
text_iast:
patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ toyaṁ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati |
tad ahaṁ bhakty-upahṛtam aśnāmi prayatātmanaḥ ||
translation_en: Whoever offers me a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water with devotion — that loving offering, made with a pure heart, I accept.

text_devanagari:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च॥
text_iast:
aham ātmā guḍākeśa sarva-bhūtāśaya-sthitaḥ |
aham ādiś ca madhyaṁ ca bhūtānām anta eva ca ||
translation_en: I am the Self, O Gudakesha, dwelling in the heart of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and the very end of all creatures.

text_devanagari:
अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।
निर्ममो निरहंकारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी॥
text_iast:
adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca |
nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣamī ||
translation_en: One who bears hatred toward no being, who is friendly and compassionate, free from possessiveness and ego, equal in pleasure and pain, and forgiving — such a devotee is dear to me.

text_devanagari:
समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम्।
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति॥
text_iast:
samaṁ sarveṣu bhūteṣu tiṣṭhantaṁ parameśvaram |
vinaśyatsv avinaśyantaṁ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati ||
translation_en: One who sees the Supreme Lord dwelling equally in all beings — the imperishable amid the perishable — that one truly sees.

text_devanagari:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे॥
text_iast:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru |
mām evaiṣyasi satyaṁ te pratijāne priyo 'si me ||
translation_en: Fix your mind on me, be devoted to me, worship me, bow to me. I truly promise — you will come to me, for you are very dear to me.

text_devanagari:
न पृथ्वी न जलं नाग्निर्न वायुर्द्यौर्न वा भवान्।
एषां साक्षिणमात्मानं चिद्रूपं विद्धि मुक्तये॥
text_iast:
na pṛthvī na jalaṁ nāgnir na vāyur dyaur na vā bhavān |
eṣāṁ sākṣiṇam ātmānaṁ cid-rūpaṁ viddhi muktaye ||
translation_en: You are not earth, not water, not fire, not air, not space. Know yourself as the witness of all these — pure consciousness — and be free.

text_devanagari:
न त्वं देहो न ते देहो भोक्ता कर्ता न वा भवान्।
चिद्रूपोऽसि सदा साक्षी निराकारः सुखं चर॥
text_iast:
na tvaṁ deho na te deho bhoktā kartā na vā bhavān |
cid-rūpo 'si sadā sākṣī nirākāraḥ sukhaṁ cara ||
translation_en: You are not the body, nor does the body belong to you. You are neither the experiencer nor the doer. You are pure consciousness, the eternal witness — formless. Live in joy.

text_devanagari:
अणोरणीयान् महतो महीयान्
आत्मास्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम्।
तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको
धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः॥
text_iast:
aṇor aṇīyān mahato mahīyān
ātmāsya jantor nihito guhāyām |
tam akratuḥ paśyati vīta-śoko
dhātu-prasādān mahimānam ātmanaḥ ||
translation_en: Subtler than the subtle, greater than the great — the Self is hidden in the cave of the heart. The desireless one, freed from grief, by the grace of the Creator, sees the majesty of the Self.

text_devanagari:
परांचि खानि व्यतृणत् स्वयम्भू-
स्तस्मात् पराङ् पश्यति नान्तरात्मन्।
कश्चिद् धीरः प्रत्यगात्मानमैक्ष-
दावृत्तचक्षुरमृतत्वमिच्छन्॥
text_iast:
parāñci khāni vyatṛṇat svayaṁbhūs
tasmāt parāṅ paśyati nāntar-ātman |
kaścid dhīraḥ pratyag-ātmānam aikṣad
āvṛtta-cakṣur amṛtatvam icchan ||
translation_en: The self-born pierced the openings of the senses outward; thus one looks outward, not at the inner Self. A rare wise one, longing for immortality, turns the gaze inward and sees the Self within.

text_devanagari:
केनेषितं पतति प्रेषितं मनः
केन प्राणः प्रथमः प्रैति युक्तः।
केनेषितां वाचमिमां वदन्ति
चक्षुः श्रोत्रं क उ देवो युनक्ति॥
text_iast:
keneṣitaṁ patati preṣitaṁ manaḥ
kena prāṇaḥ prathamaḥ praiti yuktaḥ |
keneṣitāṁ vācam imāṁ vadanti
cakṣuḥ śrotraṁ ka u devo yunakti ||
translation_en: By whose will does the mind fly to its objects? By whom is the first breath set in motion? By whose impulse are these words spoken? What deity directs the eye and ear?

text_devanagari:
सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म।
तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत।
text_iast:
sarvaṁ khalv idaṁ brahma |
taj-jalān iti śānta upāsīta |
translation_en: All this is indeed Brahman — from it all is born, into it all dissolves, and by it all breathes. Meditate on this in stillness.

text_devanagari:
सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं ब्रह्म।
यो वेद निहितं गुहायां परमे व्योमन्।
सोऽश्नुते सर्वान् कामान् सह ब्रह्मणा विपश्चिता॥
text_iast:
satyaṁ jñānam anantaṁ brahma |
yo veda nihitaṁ guhāyāṁ parame vyoman |
so 'śnute sarvān kāmān saha brahmaṇā vipaścitā ||
translation_en: Brahman is truth, knowledge, infinity. One who knows it hidden in the cave of the heart, in the highest sky — attains all desires alongside the all-knowing Brahman.

text_devanagari:
द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया
समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते।
तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्त्य-
नश्नन्नन्योऽभिचाकशीति॥
text_iast:
dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā
samānaṁ vṛkṣaṁ pariṣasvajāte |
tayor anyaḥ pippalaṁ svādy atty
anaśnann anyo 'bhicākaśīti ||
translation_en: Two birds, close companions, cling to the same tree. One eats the sweet fruits; the other watches without eating.

text_devanagari: योगश्चित्तवृत्तिनिरोधः॥
text_iast: yogaś citta-vṛtti-nirodhaḥ ||
translation_en: Yoga is the cessation of the fluctuations of the mind.

text_devanagari: तदा द्रष्टुः स्वरूपेऽवस्थानम्॥
text_iast: tadā draṣṭuḥ svarūpe 'vasthānam ||
translation_en: Then the seer abides in its own true nature.

text_devanagari:
मनोबुद्ध्यहंकारचित्तानि नाहं
न च श्रोत्रजिह्वे न च घ्राणनेत्रे।
न च व्योम भूमिर् न तेजो न वायुः
चिदानन्दरूपः शिवोऽहं शिवोऽहम्॥
text_iast:
mano-buddhy-ahaṅkāra-cittāni nāhaṁ
na ca śrotra-jihve na ca ghrāṇa-netre |
na ca vyoma bhūmir na tejo na vāyuḥ
cid-ānanda-rūpaḥ śivo 'haṁ śivo 'ham ||
translation_en: I am not mind, intellect, ego, or memory; not ear or tongue, not nose or eyes; not space, earth, fire, or wind — I am the form of consciousness-bliss: I am Shiva, I am Shiva.

text_devanagari:
भज गोविन्दं भज गोविन्दं
भज गोविन्दं मूढमते।
सम्प्राप्ते सन्निहिते काले
नहि नहि रक्षति डुकृञ्करणे॥
text_iast:
bhaja govindaṁ bhaja govindaṁ
bhaja govindaṁ mūḍha-mate |
saṁprāpte sannihite kāle
nahi nahi rakṣati ḍukṛñ-karaṇe ||
translation_en: Worship Govinda! Worship Govinda! Worship Govinda, O fool! When death draws near, no rules of grammar will save you.

text_devanagari:
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः
तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥
text_iast:
oṁ bhūr bhuvaḥ svaḥ |
tat savitur vareṇyaṁ
bhargo devasya dhīmahi |
dhiyo yo naḥ pracodayāt ||
translation_en: We meditate on the supreme radiance of the divine Sun who pervades the three worlds — may that light illuminate our intellect.

text_devanagari:
ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे
सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्।
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्
मृत्योर्मुक्षीय माऽमृतात्॥
text_iast:
oṁ tryambakaṁ yajāmahe
sugandhiṁ puṣṭi-vardhanam |
urvārukam iva bandhanān
mṛtyor mukṣīya mā 'mṛtāt ||
translation_en: We worship the three-eyed Lord Shiva, the fragrant nourisher of all. May he liberate us from death as a ripened gourd falls from the vine — and grant us immortality.

text_devanagari:
तद्वाग्विसर्गो जनताघविप्लवो
यस्मिन् प्रतिश्लोकमबद्धवत्यपि।
नामान्यनन्तस्य यशोऽङ्कितानि यत्
शृण्वन्ति गायन्ति गृणन्ति साधवः॥
text_iast:
tad-vāg-visargo janatāgha-viplavo
yasmin prati-ślokam abaddha-vaty api |
nāmāny anantasya yaśo 'ṅkitāni yat
śṛṇvanti gāyanti gṛṇanti sādhavaḥ ||
translation_en: That speech which names the glories of the Infinite Lord in every verse — even if imperfectly composed — and which the saints hear, sing, and accept: it is a flood that washes away the world's sins.

text_devanagari:
दुर्लभं त्रयमेवैतद् देवानुग्रहहेतुकम्।
मनुष्यत्वं मुमुक्षुत्वं महापुरुषसंश्रयः॥
text_iast:
durlabhaṁ trayam evaitad devānugraha-hetukam |
manuṣyatvaṁ mumukṣutvaṁ mahā-puruṣa-saṁśrayaḥ ||
translation_en: Three things are rare and obtained only by divine grace — human birth, the burning desire for liberation, and the shelter of a great soul.

text_devanagari:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित् कर्तुमर्हति॥
text_iast:
avināśi tu tad viddhi yena sarvam idaṁ tatam |
vināśam avyayasyāsya na kaścit kartum arhati ||
translation_en: Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded. No one is capable of bringing destruction to the imperishable.

text_devanagari:
य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्।
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते॥
text_iast:
ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaś cainaṁ manyate hatam |
ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṁ hanti na hanyate ||
translation_en: One who thinks this slays, and one who thinks this is slain — both fail to perceive the truth. It neither slays nor is it slain.

text_devanagari:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन।
निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान्॥
text_iast:
trai-guṇya-viṣayā vedā nis-trai-guṇyo bhavārjuna |
nirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣema ātmavān ||
translation_en: The Vedas deal with the three gunas; be free of them, O Arjuna. Beyond duality, rooted ever in pure being, free from getting and keeping — abide in the Self.

text_devanagari:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते॥
text_iast:
yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañjaya |
siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga ucyate ||
translation_en: Established in yoga, perform actions abandoning attachment, O Dhananjaya — equal in success and failure. This equanimity is called yoga.

text_devanagari:
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः।
स यत् प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते॥
text_iast:
yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ |
sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate ||
translation_en: Whatever a great person does, others follow. Whatever standard one sets, the world follows.

text_devanagari:
काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः।
महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम्॥
text_iast:
kāma eṣa krodha eṣa rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ |
mahāśano mahā-pāpmā viddhy enam iha vairiṇam ||
translation_en: It is desire, it is anger — born of the quality of passion — all-consuming and most sinful. Know this to be the enemy.

text_devanagari:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते।
तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति॥
text_iast:
na hi jñānena sadṛśaṁ pavitram iha vidyate |
tat svayaṁ yoga-saṁsiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati ||
translation_en: Nothing in this world purifies like knowledge. One who is perfected through yoga finds it within oneself in time.

text_devanagari:
श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः।
ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति॥
text_iast:
śraddhāvāl labhate jñānaṁ tat-paraḥ saṁyatendriyaḥ |
jñānaṁ labdhvā parāṁ śāntim acireṇādhigacchati ||
translation_en: With faith, devoted to it, with senses mastered — one attains knowledge. Having attained knowledge, one quickly reaches the highest peace.

text_devanagari:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता।
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः॥
text_iast:
yathā dīpo nivātastho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā |
yogino yata-cittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ ||
translation_en: As a lamp sheltered from wind does not flicker — this is the image recalled for the yogi of restrained mind, absorbed in the yoga of the Self.

text_devanagari:
शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया।
आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत्॥
text_iast:
śanaiḥ śanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛti-gṛhītayā |
ātma-saṁsthaṁ manaḥ kṛtvā na kiñcid api cintayet ||
translation_en: Gradually, with patience and intelligence, let the mind be withdrawn. Established in the Self — let one think of nothing else.

text_devanagari:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन् मुक्त्वा कलेवरम्।
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः॥
text_iast:
anta-kāle ca mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaram |
yaḥ prayāti sa mad-bhāvaṁ yāti nāsty atra saṁśayaḥ ||
translation_en: Whoever at the moment of death remembers me alone and leaves the body — comes to my state. Of this there is no doubt.

text_devanagari:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा।
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम्॥
text_iast:
yad yad vibhūtimat sattvaṁ śrīmad ūrjitam eva vā |
tat tad evāvagaccha tvaṁ mama tejo-'ṁśa-sambhavam ||
translation_en: Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or powerful — know that it is born from a spark of my splendor.

text_devanagari:
कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत् प्रवृद्धो
लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः।
ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे
येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः॥
text_iast:
kālo 'smi loka-kṣaya-kṛt pravṛddho
lokān samāhartum iha pravṛttaḥ |
ṛte 'pi tvāṁ na bhaviṣyanti sarve
ye 'vasthitāḥ praty-anīkeṣu yodhāḥ ||
translation_en: I am Time, the great destroyer of worlds, come to consume all. Even without you, all these warriors arrayed in opposing armies shall cease to be.

text_devanagari:
नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति।
गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति॥
text_iast:
nānyaṁ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṁ yadā draṣṭānupaśyati |
guṇebhyaś ca paraṁ vetti mad-bhāvaṁ so 'dhigacchati ||
translation_en: When the seer perceives no doer other than the gunas, and knows what is beyond the gunas — one attains my state.

text_devanagari:
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर् ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः।
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम्॥
text_iast:
abhayaṁ sattva-saṁśuddhir jñāna-yoga-vyavasthitiḥ |
dānaṁ damaś ca yajñaś ca svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam ||
translation_en: Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in knowledge and yoga, charity, self-restraint, worship, scripture-study, austerity, uprightness — these are the divine qualities.

text_devanagari:
जनक उवाच।
कथं ज्ञानमवाप्नोति कथं मुक्तिर्भविष्यति।
वैराग्यं च कथं प्राप्तमेतद् ब्रूहि मम प्रभो॥
text_iast:
janaka uvāca |
kathaṁ jñānam avāpnoti kathaṁ muktir bhaviṣyati |
vairāgyaṁ ca kathaṁ prāptam etad brūhi mama prabho ||
translation_en: Janaka asked: How does one attain knowledge? How does liberation come about? And how is dispassion attained? Tell me this, O Lord.

text_devanagari:
भावाभावविकारश्च स्वभावादिति निश्चयी।
निर्विकारो गतक्लेशः सुखेनैवोपशाम्यति॥
text_iast:
bhāvābhāva-vikāraś ca svabhāvād iti niścayī |
nirvikāro gata-kleśaḥ sukhenaivopaśāmyati ||
translation_en: One who is certain that all arising and all dissolving is simply the play of one's own nature — becomes free of agitation, free of affliction, and rests easily in peace.

text_devanagari:
यस्तु सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मन्येवानुपश्यति।
सर्वभूतेषु चात्मानं ततो न विजुगुप्सते॥
text_iast:
yas tu sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmany evānupaśyati |
sarva-bhūteṣu cātmānaṁ tato na vijugupsate ||
translation_en: One who sees all beings in the Self alone, and the Self in all beings — feels no revulsion toward any.

text_devanagari:
यस्मिन् सर्वाणि भूतानि आत्मैवाभूद् विजानतः।
तत्र को मोहः कः शोक एकत्वमनुपश्यतः॥
text_iast:
yasmin sarvāṇi bhūtāni ātmaivābhūd vijānataḥ |
tatra ko mohaḥ kaḥ śoka ekatvam anupaśyataḥ ||
translation_en: When for the one who knows, all beings have become the Self itself — what delusion, what grief can there be for one who sees unity?

text_devanagari:
नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो
न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन।
यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्य-
स्तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूं स्वाम्॥
text_iast:
nāyam ātmā pravacanena labhyo
na medhayā na bahunā śrutena |
yam evaisa vṛṇute tena labhyas
tasyaiṣa ātmā vivṛṇute tanūṁ svām ||
translation_en: This Self cannot be attained by study, nor by intellect, nor by much learning. The one whom it chooses — by that one alone is it attained; to that one the Self reveals its own nature.

text_devanagari:
उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत।
क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति॥
text_iast:
uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpya varān nibodhata |
kṣurasya dhārā niśitā duratyayā durgaṁ pathas tat kavayo vadanti ||
translation_en: Arise! Awake! Approach the great ones and learn. Sharp as a razor's edge, hard to traverse — so the wise declare is this path.

text_devanagari:
यच्चक्षुषा न पश्यति येन चक्षूंषि पश्यति।
तदेव ब्रह्म त्वं विद्धि नेदं यदिदमुपासते॥
text_iast:
yac cakṣuṣā na paśyati yena cakṣūṁṣi paśyati |
tad eva brahma tvaṁ viddhi nedaṁ yad idam upāsate ||
translation_en: That which the eye cannot see, but by which the eye sees — know that alone as Brahman, not what people worship here.

text_devanagari:
यस्यामतं तस्य मतं मतं यस्य न वेद सः।
अविज्ञातं विजानतां विज्ञातमविजानताम्॥
text_iast:
yasyāmataṁ tasya mataṁ mataṁ yasya na veda saḥ |
avijñātaṁ vijānatāṁ vijñātam avijānatām ||
translation_en: Those who think they do not know Brahman — know it. Those who think they know — do not know. It is unknown to those who know; known to those who say they do not know.
